era_context: Early Dynastic Period III in Mesopotamia; Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty IV). Akkadian consolidation. Indus Valley Civilization mature phase. Bronze Age in full development.

events.title: Old Kingdom Egypt Dynasty IV continued dominance
events.description: Dynasty IV maintained centralized pharaonic authority over Egypt. The state continued its monumental building programs and religious-administrative infrastructure. The Nile's annual inundation cycle provided the agricultural surplus necessary to support the state apparatus, priesthood, and construction projects.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Old Kingdom sites

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization urban centers at peak
events.description: Major urban centers such as Harappa and Mohenjo-daro were fully developed and functioning. Evidence suggests sophisticated urban planning, standardized weights and measures, and complex social organization. The undeciphered Indus script was in use on seals and pottery. The civilization's non-militaristic character (few weapons) suggests either peaceful internal organization or external security.
events.certainty_note: Radiocarbon dating places mature Indus phase c. 2600–1900 BCE.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for 2637 BCE. The year is archaeologically indistinguishable from surrounding years.
historiographic_note: This year represents a stable period in documented civilizations. The historical record is sparse and relies entirely on archaeological evidence and later compilations. No contemporary written records document 2637 BCE specifically.

graph_edges.note: Indus civilization in stable mature phase
